evals.prompt: Make me a full sprite sheet plan for a torchlight prop in a dark roguelite dungeon. I want idle flame, flare, dim, extinguish, and relight, and I want it generated with OpenAI imagegen later.
evals.expected_output: A run-ready brief with prop-specific rows, base prompt, row prompts, cell/grid recommendation, chroma-key guidance, and QA notes for flame readability.
assertions: Includes prop-specific torch animations instead of character defaults.
Mentions using $imagegen or OpenAI image generation without direct API bypass.
Includes background/alpha or chroma-key cleanup guidance.
Includes QA criteria for flame readability and frame separation.

evals.prompt: I need character ideation and a complete moveset sprite sheet for a tiny knight with a lantern spear: idle, run, jump, light attack, heavy attack, hurt, and death. Help me think through the concept first, then make the prompts.
evals.expected_output: Several compact character concepts or a recommended direction, followed by an identity-locked manifest and prompts for all requested moves.
assertions: Provides concept ideation before finalizing the sheet.
Includes all requested moves with frame counts or timing notes.
Defines identity locks for the lantern spear character.
Provides base and row prompt structure.

evals.prompt: Assume I provided a creature sketch as a reference. Turn it into a game-ready sprite sheet plan for a top-down RPG enemy, so I need walk and attack directions, hurt, and defeat. Keep the same face and markings from the reference.
evals.expected_output: A reference-aware workflow that labels input images, preserves identity and markings, chooses top-down directional rows, and blocks unsafe mirroring.
assertions: Labels the sketch as an identity reference.
Chooses top-down directional walk and attack rows.
Mentions preserving face and markings.
Warns against unsafe mirroring when asymmetry matters.

evals.prompt: Set up the folder and prompt files for a 96x96 pickup sprite called Moon Salt. It needs idle, sparkle, collect, and respawn. Don't generate images yet, just prep the run so another agent can continue.
evals.expected_output: A scaffold-only run plan that uses the bundled script or equivalent folder contract, creates/mentions manifest and prompt files, and avoids image generation.
assertions: Does not claim images were generated.
Uses or describes the sprite-runs folder contract.
Includes manifest/prompt files for idle, sparkle, collect, and respawn.
Keeps the output ready for a later agent handoff.

evals.prompt: Review this sprite sheet manifest for engine handoff: it has a 128x128 side-view character with idle 6 frames, run 8 frames, attack 8 frames, hurt 4 frames. Tell me what would break in Unity or Godot.
evals.expected_output: A validation-oriented review that checks manifest structure, frame counts, engine import concerns, timing, filtering/compression, and missing final asset metadata.
assertions: Mentions manifest validation or required manifest fields.
Includes Unity or Godot import concerns.
Checks cell size, frame counts, and animation ids.
Identifies missing file/timing/background metadata if not provided.
